name: promotion flow recalls staging before canonical

seed.title: Promotion Flow Eval
seed.summary: Promotion flow keeps staging, promotion, and canonical authority separate.
seed.body: MultiagentBrain promotion starts in staging, passes review, then writes canonical memory. Local-agent outputs enter governed capture and review paths.

name: fts vector sync health remains visible
query: How should FTS vector sync degradation be surfaced?

seed.title: FTS Vector Sync Eval
seed.summary: FTS and vector retrieval can diverge, and vector degradation must be surfaced.
seed.body: Lexical FTS fallback can still return useful results, but vector degradation should appear in retrieval health.

name: duplicate supersession stays governed
query: How are duplicate notes and supersession handled?

seed.title: Duplicate Supersession Eval
seed.summary: Duplicate detection and supersession require review before canonical state changes.
seed.body: Duplicate notes are reviewed, supersession is explicit, and canonical content is not overwritten silently.

name: thin mcp transport is not authority
query: What does thin MCP transport mean for memory writes?

seed.title: Thin MCP Transport Eval
seed.summary: MCP is a thin transport that validates input and calls the orchestrator.
seed.body: The MCP adapter does not become memory authority; it validates transport input and dispatches through orchestrator boundaries.

name: session recall stays non authoritative
sessionMessages: Session recall is non-authoritative continuity, not canonical fact memory.

seed.title: Session Recall Eval
seed.summary: Session recall is useful continuity, but it stays non-authoritative.
seed.body: Session archives are immutable recall surfaces and must not be treated as canonical facts without confirmation.

name: qwen3 coder profile is local and bounded
query: What is the qwen3-coder local profile for?

seed.title: Qwen3 Coder Profile Eval
seed.summary: qwen3-coder is a local coding profile with deterministic budget metadata.
seed.body: The qwen3-coder profile is local, uses bounded phase budgets, and does not require paid model escalation.

name: retrieval degraded health is explicit
query: What happens when vector retrieval is degraded?

seed.title: Retrieval Health Eval
seed.summary: Retrieval health should mark degraded mode when lexical fallback masks vector failure.
seed.body: Lexical results can continue in degraded health, but operators and agents must see the health state.

name: tool output spillover stays inspectable
query: Why spill over large tool output?

seed.title: Tool Output Spillover Eval
seed.summary: Tool-output spillover keeps large output inspectable while keeping prompts bounded.
seed.body: Large tool results should be persisted as spillover and represented in prompts with bounded previews.

name: local agent traces avoid hidden reasoning
query: What should local agent traces store?

seed.title: Local Agent Trace Eval
seed.summary: Local agent trace records store status, reason, request, model role, and tool metadata.
seed.body: Traces are operational records with request IDs, model roles, status, and concise reasons.

name: review gui path remains operator workflow

seed.title: Review GUI Eval
seed.summary: Operator review can use scripts/review-note-gui.py and governed review services.
seed.body: The review GUI path supports operator review; canonical promotion stays under governed approval paths.
